parts.text:
{
  "analysis": {
    "whatTheUserAskedFor": "A self-unblocking AI development team lab.",
    "deliveryGoal": "To enable the platform operator to prove that an automated 'conveyor' system can: 1) diagnose missing capabilities, 2) create new work items (stories or tool requests), 3) resume work autonomously once blockages (e.g., missing authority) are resolved, and 4) produce a product-owner review package without manual intervention.",
    "acceptanceFocus": [
      "The AI development team lab's ability to operate without manual intervention for defined 'blocking' scenarios.",
      "The 'conveyor' system's automated diagnosis of specific missing capabilities.",
      "The 'conveyor' system's automated creation of new stories or missing-tool requests in a specified system/format.",
      "The 'conveyor' system's automatic detection of and resumption of work upon the existence of necessary 'authority'.",
      "The 'conveyor' system's fully automated assembly and delivery of a product-owner review package.",
      "Measurable evidence or demonstration that these capabilities can be 'proven' by the platform operator."
    ]
  },
  "ambiguities": [
    "\"Self-unblocking AI development team lab\": The specific scope of 'self-unblocking' needs definition (what types of blockers are covered?). What are the components and boundaries of this 'lab'?",
    "\"Conveyor\": Is this synonymous with the 'AI development team lab', a specific module within it, or an external system it orchestrates? Its precise role and scope are unclear.",
    "\"Diagnose missing capabilities\": What specific types of capabilities (e.g., technical skills, tooling access, data, infrastructure, knowledge) are to be diagnosed? How is a 'diagnosis' verified or proven?",
    "\"Create the next story or missing-tool request\": What is the target system (e.g., Jira, Azure DevOps, internal ticketing) for these requests? What specific format, content, and metadata are required for these 'stories' or 'requests'?",
    "\"Resume work when authority exists\": What constitutes 'authority' in this context? How is the existence of 'authority' detected by the system? What specific 'work' is being resumed?",
    "\"Product-owner review package\": What specific content, format, and delivery mechanism are expected for this package? What level of detail and type of artifacts should it include?",
    "\"Without manual assembly\": What specific 'assembly' steps are being eliminated? Does this include data collection, report generation, dashboard updates, etc.?"
  ],
  "acceptanceRecommendations": [
    "**Define 'Self-Unblocking' Scope:** Specify a clear, initial set of 'blockers' (e.g., specific dependency issues, access requests for known systems, common configuration errors) that the lab must autonomously resolve.",
    "**Clarify 'Conveyor' Role:** Explicitly define the 'conveyor' system's responsibilities and its relationship to the 'AI development team lab'.",
    "**Specify Diagnosis Criteria:** Detail the types of 'missing capabilities' the system must diagnose (e.g., lack of specific library, missing cloud resource permission) and the observable output/evidence of such a diagnosis.",
    "**Standardize Request Creation:** Define the target system (e.g., 'our Jira instance'), the required fields, templates, and triggers for automated story or tool request creation.",
    "**Establish 'Authority' Detection:** Clearly define what signals 'authority exists' (e.g., a specific API response, a change in a linked access management system, a status update in a ticket) and how the system monitors for these signals to resume work.",
    "**Outline Review Package Content:** Provide a detailed list of expected contents, format (e.g., PDF report, dashboard link), and automated delivery method (e.g., email to product owner) for the review package.",
    "**Define 'Proof' Metrics:** Identify specific metrics or observable behaviors (e.g., dashboards showing automated diagnoses, logs of successful story creations, uptime/workflow completion rates without manual intervention) that will 'prove' the system's capabilities."
  ]
}
